mnemonics: ER-to-Relational Mapping: Remember S.A.F.E. Entities: Strong entities get their own table, Attributes become columns, Foreign keys link entities, and Everything must have a primary key.
keyPoints: Each entity (strong) becomes a table with its attributes as columns.
For a relationship, add a foreign key to the 'many' side table or create a new table for many-to-many relationships.
Composite attributes are broken down into simple columns, and multi-valued attributes become a separate table linked by a foreign key.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to create a separate table for a many-to-many relationship and trying to represent it with just foreign keys.
Incorrectly placing a foreign key on the 'one' side of a one-to-many relationship instead of the 'many' side.
Omitting the primary key constraint on the new table created for a multi-valued attribute.
